status: Repo-owned extensions to sage-safety-rules-v0.9.json. Same schema. Holds the battle-tested typed-phrase triggers migrated from the legacy lib/confidence.py _CRISIS_PATTERNS list, mapped onto the supervisor's tiers. Candidates for promotion into the next supervisor rules version (physician review covers both files).
notes: The base file's triggers are written as clinical descriptions; these are phrases patients actually type. Both files feed the same deterministic matcher. The optional co_modifiers field (used by the what's-the-point rule) requires one of the listed words within 60 characters of the trigger; the base schema does not use it.

rules.note: Legacy self_harm list. An active ingestion ('overdose', 'took too many') is expected to be raised to T1 by the AI-judgment layer; the deterministic floor keeps the warm MH response as the minimum.
High false-positive phrase ('what's the point of this lab test?'). Fires only with a despair co-modifier within 60 characters.
Apostrophe-less and paraphrase variants of the base breathing rule.

Typed-phrase hemorrhage variants. Base rule 'coughing up blood more than streaks' never appears verbatim in patient messages; deterministic floor treats any coughed blood as T1 and the AI layer can keep it there or explain streaks.
Possible spinal cord compression. Legacy list treated this as a 911-level emergency; the base file's clinical placement is T2 (care team within the hour), so it lands at T2 here.
Raised ICP / brain mets red flags. Matches the base neuro_new tier.
In cancer care this is the sentinel symptom for pneumonitis, pulmonary embolism, or cardiotoxicity. Severe variants are T1 via the base breathing rule.
Deterministic floor cannot verify the decimal or treatment status, so any stated fever holds at T2 (err toward escalation, per the base file's most important rule).
Base file places new cough without breathing trouble at T3. Cough WITH breathing trouble hits the breathing rules first (higher tier wins).
